stimulus: In 2018, a research group led by physicists Hiroshi Tanaka and Lena Volkov managed to lower the thermal conductivity of a layered crystal to a record minimum—ultraphononic insulation. An almost perfectly heat-blocking (and, as its name implies, vibration-trapping) condition, ______blank
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

answerOptions.content: ultraphononic insulation is achieved when a crystal's thermal conductivity falls below 0.05.
when its thermal conductivity falls below 0.05, a crystal achieves ultraphononic insulation.
a crystal, when its thermal conductivity falls below 0.05, achieves ultraphononic insulation.
achieving ultraphononic insulation happens when a crystal's thermal conductivity falls below 0.05.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The subject of the modifier "An almost perfectly heat-blocking…condition" is "ultraphononic insulation." Subject-modifier placement requires a modifier and its subject to be next to one another, so "ultraphononic insulation" must be the first word in the missing clause.

Choice B is incorrect. This choice creates a subject-modifier placement error. The subject of the modifier is "ultraphononic insulation," so "ultraphononic insulation" must be the first word in the missing clause. Choice C is incorrect.
